mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 0.2pp decline in 1 month
failure_condition: Positive payrolls and declining unemployment occur in > 60% of cases without corresponding SPX gains (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates expanding economic activity that raises corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with GDP growth (Pearson r < 0.1) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation remains above 2.5% for 3 months but 5-day SPX returns are positive in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO declines (Pearson r > -0.1).

mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rates (>4.5%) during monetary tightening cycles compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate improving consumer confidence and business investment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns show no significant positive correlation with HOUST increases (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer and business demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS and HOUST trends occur in > 60% of cases without corresponding SPX gains (Rank IC < 0.05).
